title: Certified CSL Model Checking via Tensor-Train Decomposition of Chemical Master Equation States

reviewers.name: Yuan Cheng
reviewers.key_strength: Error-contraction analysis exploiting the Metzler structure of CME generators provides mathematically rigorous probabilistic soundness guarantees.
reviewers.key_weakness: The clamping problem—TT-SVD producing negative probability entries—is a genuine soundness gap that requires either tight bounds or alternative non-negativity-preserving rounding.

reviewers.name: Aniruddha Sinha
reviewers.key_strength: CSL operators executing natively on MPS-compressed representations are genuinely novel algorithms with no precedent in tensor-network or verification literature.
reviewers.key_weakness: Unbounded-until fixpoint iteration may fail for slowly-mixing CTMCs with metastability, weakening the temporal logic coverage claim.

reviewers.name: Lila Zhang
reviewers.key_strength: Rank-1 TT mask encoding of axis-aligned atomic propositions enables efficient satisfaction-set algebra without state-space materialization.
reviewers.key_weakness: Non-axis-aligned predicates have exponential TT rank in the number of referenced species, limiting expressiveness for multi-species biological properties.

reviewers.name: Sara Roy
reviewers.key_strength: Three-valued semantics with explicit uncertainty quantification is a model design pattern for ML verification tools handling approximate computation.
reviewers.key_weakness: The deferred MLP rank controller represents a missed opportunity to demonstrate ML-augmented formal verification within the project scope.

reviewers.name: Joseph S. Chang
reviewers.key_strength: The complexity separation theorem (Theorem 3) provides the first rigorous proof-theoretic justification for TT-based model checking over explicit enumeration.
reviewers.key_weakness: The trusted computing base (~60K LoC) is large by formal verification standards; no component is formally verified in a proof assistant.

consensus_strengths: First system implementing CSL model-checking operators natively on tensor-train compressed state representations, a genuinely novel algorithmic contribution.
Rigorous pre-build validation gate with falsifiable go/no-go criteria on 10+ adversarial BioModels prevents wasted engineering effort.
Error-contraction analysis exploiting the Metzler structure of CME generators provides sound certified probability bounds for single-level CSL.
Complexity separation theorem (Theorem 3) provides theoretical justification for the TT approach on modular CTMCs.
consensus_weaknesses: TT-SVD clamping problem introduces a soundness gap: negative probability entries from truncation require either tight clamping bounds or non-negativity-preserving rounding.
Non-axis-aligned predicates have exponentially growing TT rank, limiting CSL expressiveness for multi-species biological constraints.
Unbounded-until fixpoint iteration may diverge for slowly-mixing CTMCs, forcing fallback to bounded-until approximation.
The ~60K LoC trusted computing base has no formally verified components, despite the system claiming certified verification.
Adaptive rank controller is a simple greedy heuristic; learned rank allocation policies are deferred despite strong potential for improvement.
actionable_recommendations: Prioritize non-negativity-preserving TT rounding (Uschmajew 2015) as the primary strategy rather than post-hoc clamping with error bounds.
Implement SAT-based exact species ordering for small networks (N ≤ 20) to benchmark heuristic ordering quality and quantify the optimality gap.
Formalize Theorem 1 (Error Contraction) in Lean 4 to reduce the TCB for the soundness-critical error certification path.
Quantify the TT rank growth for common non-axis-aligned biological predicates and provide guidance on when mask rank becomes prohibitive.
Include a pilot RL experiment for bond-dimension allocation on 2–3 BioModels to validate whether learned rank control merits full implementation.
